extracted.brandBackground: Full-service butcher shop and grocery franchise specializing in marinated meats, specialty cuts, homemade prepared foods, fine cheeses, wine and beer, and food preparation classes, founded in 2020 with one affiliate-owned location operating since 2013.
extracted.conceptRationale: Primarily a retail butcher shop and specialty grocery selling marinated meats, prepared foods, cheeses, and wine/beer — a product-retail concept, not a restaurant or entertainment venue.
extracted.staffingRationale: Item 15 requires the franchisee or a designated manager to be on-site daily, and Item 11 training covers hiring, scheduling, and managing hourly employees, managers, and assistant managers.

leadership.name: Greg Snyder
leadership.background: Founder and Managing Member since inception (June 2020); also founder and President of affiliate Southern Steer Butcher, LLC since September 2012 with over 25 years in restaurant and hospitality.
leadership.whyItMatters: Sole operator of the only proven affiliate location and primary architect of the system.

leadership.name: Jacques Lapointe
leadership.background: Managing Member since inception (June 2020); previously Founder and Chairman of JAN-PRO Franchising International from 2005 to 2020.
leadership.whyItMatters: Brings large-scale franchise system-building experience from a major commercial cleaning franchise.

leadership.name: Wali Bacdayan
leadership.background: Managing Member since June 2022; serves on multiple corporate boards and is a venture partner and founding investor in several companies.
leadership.whyItMatters: Provides capital markets and board-level governance experience to the growing franchise.

leadership.name: Matt Kenagy
leadership.background: Fractional CMO since May 2024 via House of Hardway, LLC; prior CMO at GamePlus, Inc. and Director of Loyalty Marketing at Open Network Exchange.
leadership.whyItMatters: Leads brand marketing and loyalty strategy for franchisee growth.

leadership.name: Meghan Reynolds
leadership.background: Franchise Support Manager since August 2022; previously Administrator at Southern Steer Butcher, LLC from 2013 to 2022.
leadership.whyItMatters: Primary franchisee onboarding and operational support contact with deep brand history.

leadership.name: John O'Brien
leadership.role: Director of Operations and Business Coaching
leadership.background: Director of Operations and Business Coaching since July 2020; previously Strategic Implementation and Productivity Lead since inception.
leadership.whyItMatters: Oversees operational standards and franchisee business coaching across the system.

item19.notes: Five locations met reporting conditions (operated full calendar year 2025, at least one year open, financials submitted): one affiliate-owned (Clearwater, FL) and four franchised (Sarasota, Ocoee, St. Petersburg, Jacksonville). Net Income figures are before owner's compensation and exclude income taxes, payroll taxes, travel, owner benefits, bonuses, amortization, interest, and disability/health insurance. Imputed royalty adjustments shown separately in Charts 1B, 2B, 2C.

cohorts.label: Affiliate Location - Clearwater (Gross Sales)
cohorts.basis: Total gross sales for the Clearwater affiliate location for the full year January 1 – December 31, 2025.

cohorts.label: Affiliate Location - Clearwater (Net Income Before Owner Compensation)
cohorts.basis: Net income before owner compensation after deducting COGS, labor, occupancy, marketing, technology, operating, insurance, and miscellaneous expenses; royalty not paid (imputed separately in Chart 1B at $109,451.30, yielding adjusted net income of $214,467.07).

cohorts.label: Franchised Locations - Sarasota (Gross Sales)
cohorts.basis: Total gross sales for the Sarasota franchised location for full year 2025.

cohorts.label: Franchised Locations - Ocoee (Gross Sales)
cohorts.basis: Total gross sales for the Ocoee franchised location for full year 2025.

cohorts.label: Franchised Locations - St. Petersburg (Gross Sales)
cohorts.basis: Total gross sales for the St. Petersburg franchised location for full year 2025.

cohorts.label: Franchised Locations - Jacksonville (Gross Sales)
cohorts.basis: Total gross sales for the Jacksonville franchised location for full year 2025.

cohorts.label: Franchised Locations - Sarasota (Net Income Before Owner Compensation)
cohorts.basis: Net income before owner compensation for Sarasota 2025; adjusted figure after imputed additional royalty is $314,390.68.

cohorts.label: Franchised Locations - Ocoee (Net Income Before Owner Compensation)
cohorts.basis: Net income before owner compensation for Ocoee 2025; adjusted figure after imputed additional royalty is $142,108.05.

cohorts.label: Franchised Locations - St. Petersburg (Net Income Before Owner Compensation)
cohorts.basis: Net income before owner compensation for St. Petersburg 2025; no separate imputed adjustment disclosed.

cohorts.label: Franchised Locations - Jacksonville (Net Income Before Owner Compensation)
cohorts.basis: Net loss before owner compensation for Jacksonville 2025; negative result at -4.1% net margin.

lineItems.notes: Paid to franchisor at signing; non-refundable.

lineItems.category: Travel and Living Expenses for Initial Training (Two Weeks)
lineItems.notes: Paid to airlines, car rental, hotels, restaurants during training.
Paid to franchisor after completing Initial Training Program.
Paid to architect.
Paid to landlord.
Paid to utility and phone companies.
Build-out of 1,500–2,000 sq ft space to meet Southern Steer standards.
Estimated lease rates $3,500–$8,500/month; three months shown here.

lineItems.category: Inventory and Supplies for Three Months
lineItems.notes: Initial inventory for first three months of operation.
Includes refrigeration, display cases, prep/cooking equipment, freezers, smallwares.
Includes digital menu signage for first year.
Hardware, software, wireless network, POS scales, printers, modems; 50% due on order, 50% after installation.
$720/month for cloud-based support; three months shown.
Includes liquor license, manager and food handler certifications.
$311/month for IFX, email, social media, Google Business; three months shown.

lineItems.category: Direct Ad TV Subscription Fee for First Year
lineItems.notes: Annual fee covering three direct TV ads.
Includes initial IIF Importer App annual subscription.
$30/month billed quarterly by vendor.
Three months of required general liability, workers comp, and property insurance.
Paid to franchisor; includes tent, business cards, rack cards, giveaways.
Must be spent within first 90 days of operations.
Working capital for payroll, benefits, admin, janitorial, maintenance, additional inventory, utilities.

hiddenCosts.name: QuickBooks Subscription
hiddenCosts.description: Required accounting software estimated at $80/month, not listed as a named fee in Item 6 but disclosed in Item 11.

hiddenCosts.name: Customer Loyalty Program Cost
hiddenCosts.description: Mandatory participation in franchisor-designated loyalty/gift card program; Clearwater affiliate spent $129,529 and franchised locations spent $54,133–$130,865 in 2025.

hiddenCosts.name: Minimum Grand Opening Marketing Spend
hiddenCosts.description: Franchisee must spend at least $15,000 on grand opening advertising within first 90 days, in addition to ongoing local advertising obligation.

hiddenCosts.name: Local Advertising Requirement (1% of Gross Revenue)
hiddenCosts.description: Annual obligation to spend 1% of gross revenue on local advertising, separate from Brand Fund Contribution.

hiddenCosts.name: Brand Conference Fee
hiddenCosts.description: Mandatory annual conference attendance; up to $1,500 per attendee plus travel and living expenses.

hiddenCosts.name: POS System Upgrade Costs
hiddenCosts.description: Ongoing technology upgrades estimated at $1,000–$5,000 with no contractual frequency cap.

hiddenCosts.name: Minimum Royalty Payment
hiddenCosts.description: Franchisee must pay minimum royalty regardless of sales level; failure may result in termination.

hiddenCosts.name: Management Fee (Step-In)
hiddenCosts.description: 50% of gross revenues payable if franchisee dies, becomes permanently disabled, or abandons the business — in addition to royalty and brand fund.

hiddenCosts.name: Liquidated Damages for Unauthorized Products
hiddenCosts.description: $250 per day for each day unauthorized products or services are offered or sold.

operationalRisks.title: Franchisor Financial Condition Risk
operationalRisks.description: The FDD's Special Risks section explicitly states the franchisor's financial condition calls into question its ability to provide services and support to franchisees.
operationalRisks.source: Special Risks, p. iii; Item 21, p. 54

operationalRisks.title: Mandatory Minimum Sales Requirements
operationalRisks.description: Franchisee must achieve $1,000,000 in Year 1 rising to $1,700,000 by Year 5+; failure can result in territory reduction, additional competition in Protected Area, or termination.

operationalRisks.title: Mandatory Minimum Royalty Payments
operationalRisks.description: Royalties must be paid regardless of sales levels; inability to pay may result in franchise termination and loss of investment.
operationalRisks.source: Special Risks, p. iii; Item 6, p. 6

operationalRisks.title: Spousal Liability Guarantee
operationalRisks.description: Franchisee's spouse must sign a personal guarantee making them liable for all financial obligations under the franchise agreement even without an ownership interest.
operationalRisks.source: Special Risks, p. iii; Item 15, p. 36

operationalRisks.title: Out-of-State Dispute Resolution
operationalRisks.description: All disputes must be resolved by mediation, arbitration, and/or litigation exclusively in Florida, which may disadvantage out-of-state franchisees.
operationalRisks.source: Special Risks, p. iii; Item 17, p. 41

operationalRisks.title: Non-Exclusive Territory
operationalRisks.description: Franchisee does not receive an exclusive territory; franchisor reserves broad rights to compete via alternative channels, wholesale, internet, food trailers, and other brands within the Protected Area.

operationalRisks.title: Small and Early-Stage System
operationalRisks.description: Only 9 total units open as of fiscal year-end 2025 (8 franchised, 1 affiliate), with limited franchisee validation data and only 5 locations meeting Item 19 reporting conditions.

operationalRisks.title: Liquor License Risk
operationalRisks.description: Failure to obtain or retain a liquor license could adversely affect the business, as wine and beer sales are a component of the concept.

operationalRisks.title: Franchisor Right to Modify System Without Consent
operationalRisks.description: Franchisor may change the Brand Manual, system standards, and required products at any time, potentially requiring additional franchisee expenditures.
operationalRisks.source: Item 11, p. 27; General Risks, p. ii

operationalRisks.title: No Franchisee Right to Terminate
operationalRisks.description: Franchisee has no contractual right to terminate the Franchise Agreement for any reason.

operationalRisks.title: Marks Owned by Affiliate, Not Franchisor
operationalRisks.description: The trademarks are owned by affiliate Southern Steer Butcher, LLC under a license agreement; if that license is disrupted, franchisee rights could be affected.

operationalRisks.title: One Jacksonville Location Reported a Net Loss
operationalRisks.description: The Jacksonville franchised location reported a net loss of $46,118 before owner compensation in 2025, demonstrating that not all locations are profitable.

rentDetail.source: Item 7, p. 13 (estimated lease rates $3,500–$8,500/month for 1,500–2,000 sq ft)

financialCondition.auditorName: RPF CPA Accounting Services (Ferland and Company CPAs)

riskReasons: 3 high-severity operational tripwires disclosed — see the tripwires section.
Franchisor financial distress disclosed (negative net worth) — confirm its ability to support the system against the audited statements.
Franchisee unit economics could not be scored — no usable Item 19 (company/affiliate-owned only, or no FPR). Treat this as UNVERIFIED, not low-risk.
notes: Average rent not found; fixed costs may be understated.
No Item 19 middle-cohort or network-average revenue found; economics are indeterminate.

underwriting.assessment: With $250,000 liquid against a $586,783 mid-point build-out, there is a $336,783 capital gap. An SBA loan or an equity partner is required to do this deal.

insights.conceptRationale: Primarily a retail butcher shop and specialty grocery selling marinated meats, prepared foods, cheeses, and wine/beer — a product-retail concept, not a restaurant or entertainment venue.

benchmark.label: Retail / product
benchmark.dominantRisk: Gross margin on product (high COGS) and inventory turns. Thin margins amplify any sales miss.
benchmark.rampNote: 3–12 months to run-rate depending on category.
considerations: Budget COGS at 45–65% of sales — product margin is the whole game here.
Verify inventory turns and markdown/shrink rates with Item 20 operators.
Confirm minimum inventory and required reorder commitments in Item 8/Item 11.

crossCheck.message: No operating-EBITDA margin is disclosed in Item 19. The 5–12% band below is an industry estimate for this concept — validate it against real franchisee P&Ls (Item 20) before relying on any margin.

assumptions.detail: Classified as “Retail / product” from the FDD (Item 1 / brand description).

insights.consultCtaPitch: These are category benchmarks applied to the disclosed top line — your unit's real costs will differ, and that difference is the conversation. Want them pressure-tested against actual franchisee P&Ls for your territory?
insights.asOf: 2026 — general industry benchmarks, refine against real franchisee P&Ls
insights.disclaimer: Industry benchmark ranges for your own budgeting and Item 20 questions — NOT a projection of this franchise's results, and not investment advice.

financialCondition.headline: This franchisor is carrying a deficit and running a net loss as it grows — net worth is roughly $112K in the red. Its audit is clean and revenue is rising, so the question is runway: understand how the gap is funded, and for how long, before you commit.
financialCondition.context: Worth perspective: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt; revenue is growing. Early-stage franchisors commonly run losses and carry a deficit while investing to scale — this reads more like spending ahead of revenue than a failing business. The real question is runway: how the gap is funded, and for how long.
body: What we found (2025-09-30, audited by RPF CPA Accounting Services (Ferland and Company CPAs)): Net loss of $549K on $618K of revenue — roughly 1.9x spent per $1 earned. Losses are widening, not shrinking. Negative net worth (a members'/stockholders' deficit) of $112K, and deepening year over year. Total liabilities are about 1.1x total assets. Cash of $100K against $595K of obligations due within a year (a current ratio of 1.01). Note: about 99% of those liabilities is deferred revenue — prepaid franchise fees the company owes as future services, not borrowed money. Netting it out, liabilities are still about 0.0x assets.
Why it matters to you: field support, training, technology, and marketing come out of the franchisor's budget. If outside funding slows, those are the first things to get cut.

Verify before you commit: ask for the most recent interim (unaudited) financials and the current-year funding plan; ask whether the parent will sign a guarantee of performance; and cross-check Item 20 — unit closures, transfers, and terminations are the operational tell that corroborates financial stress.
aggravators: negative net worth that is deepening year over year
mitigants: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt
financialCondition.evidenceNote: Flagged on the FDD's "Special Risks" page and grounded in the Item 21 / Exhibit F audited financials. Figures are extracted; all ratios and grading are computed. Confirm against the source statements and a CPA before relying on them.
